summary: Iter 9 verifies (a) 3-role RBAC (Super Admin / Editor / Viewer), (b) Super Admin required-validation bypass, and (c) bidirectional sync of Employee.status → deceasedEmployees / retirements collections. ALL 7 reviewed features PASS end-to-end via Playwright as the actual super_admin, editor1, and viewer1 logins. The only minor issue is a missing 'Saved successfully' toast on the empty-record save (record persists but no visible confirmation toast within 6s) and the user-form password field is rendered as type=text instead of type=password.

ui_bugs.issue: Password input is type='text' instead of type='password' — credentials are visible in clear text while typing/displayed. Minor security/UX concern.

After saving an EMPTY employee record, the .app-toast 'Saved successfully' confirmation is not present within 6 seconds (toast may have been auto-dismissed too quickly or never shown). Row IS persisted (visible in table immediately) — so this is UX feedback only.

verifications_passed: TEST 1 — Super Admin BYPASS required validation: nav-employees → add-employees-btn → modal shows green alert 'Super Admin: required-field validation is bypassed — you may save partial entries.' Form has novalidate=true. Click save-record-btn with all fields empty → #dt-employees row count goes 3 → 4. New empty row visible in DataTable (verified by screenshot).

TEST 2 — Users module 3-role select: Role dropdown options EXACTLY: [viewer::'Viewer (read-only)', editor::'Editor (add/edit, no delete, locked sanctioned)', super_admin::'Super Admin (full access)']. Order and values match spec. Created Editor User (editor1@iaad.gov.in / Editor@12345) and Viewer User (viewer1@iaad.gov.in / Viewer@12345); both appear in #dt-users with correct role badges 'editor' and 'viewer'. Admin session was NOT logged out during creation (secondary Firebase app worked).

TEST 3 — EDITOR role: After sign-in as editor1, sidebar HIDES nav-users and nav-auditLogs (offsetParent=null) while keeping all other modules. Offices Add modal shows blue alert 'Editor role: sanctioned posts, seniority and auto-calculated fields are locked.' The 'sanctioned' input is disabled+readOnly with label 'Sanctioned Posts * LOCKED'. Fields name/ofcadmId/departmentId/district/division remain editable. Employees Add modal: 'seniority' (label 'Seniority Number LOCKED') and 'retirementDate' (label 'Retirement Date (auto) LOCKED') are disabled+readOnly while 'name' is editable. Actions column renders ONLY view + edit data-testid buttons (no trash/delete).

TEST 4 — VIEWER role: viewer1 sidebar shows Dashboard, Employees, Postings, Offices, Departments, Retirements, Death Register but HIDES Users + Audit Logs (screenshot confirms). Toolbar on Employees page contains ONLY Excel + PDF export buttons — no Add, no Bulk Import. Actions column contains only the view (eye) icon — no edit, no delete. View modal opens cleanly on click.

TEST 5 — Bidirectional sync (Voluntary Retirement): Edited employee 'Remark Test' (RMK001), set status='Voluntary Retirement', saved. Vacate-post modal appeared, clicked vacate-no-btn. Navigated nav-retirements → new row present: 'Remark Test | RMK001 | 31 Jan 2045 | (auto from employee status change) | Voluntary Retirement'. orderNumber EXACTLY equals '(auto from employee status change)'.

TEST 6 — Bidirectional sync (Deceased): Edited another Working employee (the empty bypass-saved record), set status='Deceased', deathDate=2026-06-28, saved. Navigated nav-deceased → new row visible (screenshot confirmed) with Death Date 28 Jun 2026 and Order No. '(auto from employee status change)'. Death Register now has 2 rows (previous मुनेन्द्र शर्मा SPN/TEST/01 + new auto row).

TEST 7 — Regression: Status dropdown contains expected options ['','Working','Retired','Deceased','Promoted','Suspended','Voluntary Retirement','Terminated']. Auto-retirement popup handled via 'Close for now'. Dashboard, sidebar and topbar render cleanly. Only console warning observed is the expected pre-login 'FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions' before sign-in (cosmetic — not blocking).
critical_code_review_comments: User form: <input name=password type='text'> exposes the password while typing. Should be type='password' (with an optional show/hide toggle). File: index.html openUserForm() / renderFormField user-role overrides.

Empty-employee bypass save persists silently — no '.app-toast.ok' confirmation visible in DOM 1-6s after save. Consider always showing a success toast in saveRecord() for super_admin bypass branch (or extending toast TTL) so the admin gets feedback that an intentionally-empty record was saved.

Editor role still sees the 'Bulk Import' button on list pages — request only forbids it for Viewers, so this is fine, but reconfirm intent: Editors can bulk-import employees, which could bypass per-field LOCKED rules (sanctioned/seniority/retirementDate) if the importer doesn't apply isFieldLocked() filtering. Recommend validating bulk-import rows against PROTECTED_FIELDS for editor uploads.

Empty-name employee row in #dt-employees has no Manav Sampada ID and shows blank cells — fine for a bypass-test artifact, but in production a super_admin could create unsearchable/unlistable rows. Consider at least requiring employeeId even with bypass, or add a visible 'incomplete' badge.

Legacy 'user' → 'editor' on-the-fly normalization not directly exercised (no legacy user fixture in seed), but Users DataTable shows only editor/viewer/super_admin badges as expected. Worth a unit test.
action_items: Optional: change user-form password input to type='password' with show/hide toggle.
Optional: ensure '.app-toast.ok' toast fires (and persists ≥3s) when super_admin bypass-saves an empty/partial record so the admin has visible confirmation.
Optional: apply PROTECTED_FIELDS filtering in the bulk-import path for editor role (parity with single-record save).
No retest required — all spec items are working as designed.

success_rate.frontend: 7/7 reviewed features PASS; 2 minor UX/security notes

test_credentials: admin@iaad.gov.in / Admin@12345 (super_admin); editor1@iaad.gov.in / Editor@12345 (editor — created by this run); viewer1@iaad.gov.in / Viewer@12345 (viewer — created by this run)
seed_data_creation: Created users editor1@iaad.gov.in (editor) and viewer1@iaad.gov.in (viewer) via Users module. Added one EMPTY employee record via super_admin bypass (no fields filled) — later marked Deceased to satisfy sync test. Changed Remark Test (RMK001) status from Working → Voluntary Retirement which auto-created a retirements row. Changed empty-bypass employee status to Deceased which auto-created a deceasedEmployees row. State now: 4 users (admin, Super Admin, Editor User, Viewer User), 4 employees (मुनेन्द्र शर्मा, Remark Test, empty/bypass row, Enhanced Test), 2 retirements (1 pre-existing + 1 auto-from-status), 2 deceased (1 pre-existing + 1 auto-from-status).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter-9 RBAC + bypass + bidirectional-sync features are working. If future iterations alter the RBAC matrix, the helpers canEdit(), canDelete(), canEditSanctioned(), canManageUsers(), and PROTECTED_FIELDS in index.html are the source of truth. Three test users now exist; reuse credentials above instead of re-creating. The Remark Test / empty-bypass employees have already been mutated to retired/deceased — pick a different Working employee for any new sync tests (e.g., create one via super_admin Add).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs were RCA'd; the iteration is essentially a feature-additions iteration and all assertions pass. The only RCA-worthy observation is that on super_admin bypass-save, the success toast is either suppressed or auto-dismissed too quickly to be queried 1-6s post-save (record IS persisted). Likely cause: saveRecord() may skip the toast call when checkValidity is bypassed for admin, or the toast is hidden by the closing modal animation. Quick fix: emit the toast OUTSIDE the modal hide callback and ensure a minimum 3-4s TTL.
